name: Johnson v. United States
summary: Warrantless searches are per se unreasonable under Fourth Amendment subject only to few specifically established exceptions; neutral magistrate must evaluate probable cause, not police.
holding: Point of Fourth Amendment is to interpose neutral magistrate between police and citizens; officers' determination of probable cause insufficient to justify warrantless entry.
relevance_to_audit: Establishes requirement for neutral decision-maker in searches and enforcement actions; relevant to administrative inspections and regulatory searches without independent oversight.
